videos.title: UK | 28th April, Tuesday - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
UK | 28th April, Tuesday - RIFT Presentation 28/04/26

🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

You don’t need to be a professional trader or understand complex charts. RIFT does the heavy lifting, helping you participate in markets with speed, structure, and efficiency.

📊 Built for the modern era of trading, where automation and algorithmic execution dominate over manual trading.

⚠️ Disclaimer: Trading involves risk. Always understand fees, leverage, and market conditions before trading. 28/04/26

Run the Hermes AI Agent in Aion UI (100% Free) — Multi‑Agent Dashboard Setup Guide

The video shows how to run the Hermes AI agent inside Aion UI for free instead of using a terminal, highlighting Aion UI as a free, open-source dashboard for managing multiple agents like Hermes, Claude Code, OpenClaw, Gemini CLI, and others. It walks through downloading Aion UI from GitHub releases on Mac/Windows/Linux, opening the app, and verifying Hermes is connected and responding. The script covers key features including switching models, searching conversations, attaching folders/documents for project context, installing agent skills via a marketplace, configuring MCP tools, speech-to-text and image models, setting permissions for agents like Claude Code, and scheduling/automating tasks across multiple agents while keeping them awake. It also demonstrates remote access options (including from a phone), a desktop “pet” overlay, and creating agent teams with a leader agent coordinating others, then closes by promoting the AI Profit Boardroom community for training and coaching.

videos.title: 28.4: Draisaitl scores 2 goals to keep Oilers alive 🚨
videos.summary_source_text:
28.4: Draisaitl scores 2 goals to keep Oilers alive 🚨 Leon Draisaitl tips Evan Bouchard's shot into the net in the first period before firing in a 2nd period power play goal to give the Edmonton Oilers a 4-1 lead over the Anaheim Ducks, staving off elimination. Leon Draisaitl joined Connor McDavid as the second player in Oilers history to record 20 points when facing elimination.

videos.title: Dallas Stars Pushed To Brink Of Elimination, Lose Game 5 To Minnesota Wild 4-2 | DLLS STARS POSTGAME
videos.summary_source_text:
Dallas Stars Pushed To Brink Of Elimination, Lose Game 5 To Minnesota Wild 4-2 | DLLS STARS POSTGAME Take advantage of our Playoff Diehard Sale and join our community today! https://alldlls.com/stars-playoff-offer-youtube 

The Dallas Stars post season hopes took a major setback tonight as they lost Game 5 to the Minnesota Wild 4-2 at American Airlines Center. The Stars never led in the game, but were tied at 1-1 after the first period as Miro Heiskanen’s power play goal canceled out Mats Zuccarello’s early tally. Matt Boldy scored a power play goal late in the second period and then Michael McCarron scored in the third to give the Wild a two-goal lead. Jason Robertson scored for the fifth-straight game in the series, setting a new franchise record, but Kirill Kaprizov added an empty net goal to finish off any hopes of a Stars comeback. Game 6 will be played on Thursday night, April 30th, start time still TBD. videos.title: Me with chop sticks... 😭
videos.summary_source_text:
Me with chop sticks... 😭 Dallas Stars forward Mavrik Bourque finds himself with a wide open net after Wild goalie Jesper Wallstedt is caught out of position, but chaos takes over in front.

videos.title: Square the Club Face for Better Lag #golfswing #golftips
videos.summary_source_text:
Square the Club Face for Better Lag #golfswing #golftips Many golfers struggle to hold lag and think it’s just a wrist or arm angle issue. The truth is that an open club face ruins lag before it even starts.
To retain lag effectively, square the club face, allow proper forearm rotation, and flex the wrist naturally while controlling the pivot. Proper sequencing of the body will naturally create lag — but you’ll only hit good shots if the face is presented correctly at impact.
Follow for more precise, practical coaching from Me and My Golf. Many golfers struggle to hold lag and think it’s just a wrist or arm angle issue. The truth is that an open club face ruins lag before it even starts.
To retain lag effectively, square the club face, allow proper forearm rotation, and flex the wrist naturally while controlling the pivot. Proper sequencing of the body will naturally create lag — but you’ll only hit good shots if the face is presented correctly at impact.

videos.title: STACKING MOMENTUM! How the Carolina Hurricanes can Go Another Level In Round 2
videos.summary_source_text:
STACKING MOMENTUM! How the Carolina Hurricanes can Go Another Level In Round 2 While the Carolina Hurricanes still wait for their Round 1 opponent, let's discuss what hopefully happens in Round 2, regardless of who they face. On the Wednesday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach starts the show by sharing his thoughts on how the Hurricanes' power play needs to refind it's form after finishing 4th in the league during the regular season. As of now, it's tied for 11th at 13.3% with the Philadelphia Flyers. Regarding the penalty kill, it's humming along as the second-best in the playoffs. Can the man-advantage get to that level next round? Secondly, Zach shares his thoughts on how he's looking at the top line to make its mark on the postseason, after combining for five points in Round 1. While they have a proven track record of being clutch, the hope is that it breaks out big time to lead the way. Finally, Zach concludes the show by giving his thoughts on who he is looking at early in terms of who to watch for Round 2. While he will give his overall analysis for the Round 2 crossover, it's never too early to scout ahead. All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

videos.title: Nathan MacKinnon NOT a Finalist For the Ted Lindsay. Are His Fellow NHLers Bored With Him?
videos.summary_source_text:
Nathan MacKinnon NOT a Finalist For the Ted Lindsay. Are His Fellow NHLers Bored With Him? Colorado Avalanche fans are stunned as Nathan MacKinnon, who led the NHL in goals and even-strength points—matching Wayne Gretzky-era levels—gets snubbed as a Ted Lindsay Award finalist. Can MacKinnon turn this slight into fuel for another dominant playoff run?

Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson debate the surprise omission, break down the award voting trends, and compare MacKinnon’s season to stars like Connor McDavid, Nikita Kucherov, and Macklin Celebrini. The conversation shifts to Avalanche Round 1 player stats, spotlighting standout performances by Artturi Lehkonen and Cale Makar, while examining who needs to step up offensively in the next round. Plus, an NHL overtime goal controversy sparks talk of much-needed rule changes. Will the Avalanche harness this chip-on-the-shoulder energy as they await their next playoff opponent?

With Series Tied 2-2, Dallas Stars Face PIVOTAL Game 5 vs. Minnesota Wild | DLLS STARS PREGAME Take advantage of our Playoff Diehard Sale and join our community today! https://alldlls.com/stars-playoff-offer-youtube 

This first round series between the Dallas Stars and the Minnesota Wild is very evenly matched, thus there should be no surprise that each team has won two games out of the first four, including each winning once on the road. The series will not be decided tonight, but the winner of Game 5 will have a big statistical advantage. Nearly 80 percent of all Stanley Cup Playoff series tied 2-2 end up going to the winner of Game 5 (239-62, 0.794). For Dallas, they will be without Nils Lundkvist, who suffered a nasty facial laceration from a skate blade during Game 4. Ilya Lyubushkin will check into the lineup to take Lundkvist’s place. For Minnesota, both Mats Zuccarello and Yakov Trenin are listed as game-time decisions. Zuccarello hasn’t played since Game 1, while Trenin was injured in Game 2. videos.title: Fitzpatrick brothers make history + PGA TOUR returns to Miami  | The Drop
videos.summary_source_text:
Fitzpatrick brothers make history + PGA TOUR returns to Miami  | The Drop The Drop relives all the excitement from the Big Easy as the Fitzpatrick brothers made history at the Zurich Classic of New Orleans by being the first pair of brothers to win the event. Alex Fitzpatrick earned his PGA TOUR card with the win, while Matt earned his third win in his last four starts. Every Tuesday returns with Matt sitting down with Billy Horschel and Tom Hoge ahead of the team event. The TOUR heads back to Miami for the first time in a decade for the Cadillac Championship, as Alexandra O’Laughlin talks to Grammy-Award DJ & Producer DJ Khaled. We’ll also take a look at which hole players need to play well on if they want to be victorious in the 305. And finally, we watch some of golf’s hardest moments at Trump National Doral.

00:40 Fitzpatrick bros dominate Zurich Classic 
01:58 FedExCup Standings update

videos.title: Episode 44: Islanders Post-Mortem | Tri-State Hockey Podcast
videos.summary_source_text:
Episode 44: Islanders Post-Mortem | Tri-State Hockey Podcast Episode 44 of the Tri-State Hockey Podcast is a full New York Islanders postmortem as Jonny Lazarus, Arthur Staple, and Mike Rupp break down a season that started with promise and ended in collapse. From Matthew Schaefer’s historic rookie campaign to Ilya Sorokin masking deeper roster flaws, the guys dive into what went wrong and why this team couldn’t sustain success. They debate the impact of Pete DeBoer behind the bench, whether this core has hit its ceiling, and what changes are actually realistic this offseason. A packed voicemail segment fuels discussion around fixing the blue line, navigating roster logjams, and finding the right fit for Mathew Barzal. It’s an honest, in-depth look at where the Islanders go next in a pivotal summer.

Chapters:
0:00 Intro & Islanders Postmortem Setup
1:00 Reflecting on the Season After Time to Process
3:00 Matthew Schaefer’s Breakout & Rookie Records
5:00 Sorokin’s Elite Season & Team Masking Issues
7:30 Deadline Moves & Questionable Decisions
10:00 Late Season Collapse & Missed Playoffs
13:00 Islanders vs Playoff Teams Comparison
16:00 Identity Issues & System Breakdown
19:00 Lack of Progress Throughout the Season
22:00 Offensive Decline & Defensive Problems
25:00 Are Sorokin’s Prime Years Being Wasted?
28:00 Pete DeBoer Impact & Expectations
31:00 Will the Islanders Actually Make Big Changes?
34:00 Voicemail #1: Fixing the Right Side Defense
41:00 Voicemail #2: Left Side Logjam & Trade Options
48:00 Voicemail #3: Who Fits with Mathew Barzal?
55:00 Should the Islanders Consider Moving Barzal?
1:02:00 Final Thoughts on Roster Direction
1:10:00 Closing Thoughts, Outlook & Wrap Up

videos.title: What's At Stake For The Oilers? | Real Kyper & Bourne
videos.summary_source_text:
What's At Stake For The Oilers? | Real Kyper & Bourne Nick Kypreos, Justin Bourne, and Sam McKee check in on the latest news regarding Mats Sundin and the Toronto Maple Leafs' GM search. Then, Stanley Cup champion Brad May comes on to discuss the vibe in Buffalo with the Sabres one game away from advancing to the second round. Later, CEO of Clear Sight Analytics and former NHL goaltender Steve Valiquette joins to discuss Linus Ullmark's playoff performance and what his future may look like in Ottawa.

videos.title: Nuggets Stay Alive, Avalanche Await Round 2, and the Rockies are Red Hot  | After the Horn
videos.summary_source_text:
Nuggets Stay Alive, Avalanche Await Round 2, and the Rockies are Red Hot  | After the Horn The latest episode of After the Horn is packed with major storylines from Denver sports and beyond. The Denver Nuggets keep their playoff hopes alive against the Timberwolves, the Colorado Avalanche wait for their second-round opponent, and the Colorado Rockies continue their surprising hot start.

Plus, the crew dives into the growing Mike Vrabel controversy, Broncos headlines, celebrity sports culture, LIV Golf drama, and why modern sports coverage has changed forever.

From playoff pressure to front-office decisions and viral off-field stories, this is smart, spirited, no-BS sports talk that starts when the buzzer sounds. Subscribe now for more from Guerilla Sports.

videos.title: The Crosby Dangle Pattern
videos.summary_source_text:
The Crosby Dangle Pattern The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

Jonah Coleman's Potential, Why Draft Class Should EXCITE Broncos Country & More | Broncos X & O's Fresh off the Denver Broncos’ 2026 NFL Draft, Broncos X & O’s breaks down why Jonah Coleman could be a perfect Sean Payton fit and why Broncos Country should be excited about Denver’s newest draft class. From Coleman’s tough running style, pass protection, and fit alongside Bo Nix to standout traits from the rest of the rookie group, this episode dives into how these picks can shape the Broncos’ future. Dave Logan and Bennie Fowler also share big-picture reactions on what Jonah Coleman, Tyler Onyedim, Kage Casey, Justin Joly and more could mean for the Denver Broncos heading into 2026. This is a must-watch for fans looking for Broncos draft analysis, Jonah Coleman insight and Bo Nix discussion all in one place.

Dave Logan and Bennie Fowler also share big-picture reactions on what Jonah Coleman, Tyler Onyedim, Kage Casey, Justin Joly and more could mean for the Denver Broncos heading into 2026. This is a must-watch for fans looking for Broncos draft analysis, Jonah Coleman insight and Bo Nix discussion all in one place.

Andre Adams reveals three BIG TIME recruits he wants to play with at Colorado On today’s DNVR Buffs Podcast, the squad discusses what four-star quarterback and Colorado commit Andre Adams said about the players he wants to recruit to play with him at Colorado. Ryan Koenigsberg, Jake Schwanitz and Scott Procter discuss what Andre Adams said to Rivals about his recruitment and commitment to Colorado and more. The latest on Colorado Football recruiting as more official visits for this summer are announced. videos.title: Don’t Contract, Relax!
videos.summary_source_text:
Don’t Contract, Relax! Power skating tells you to sit low in a chair, tense your muscles and push as hard as possible to stride.

Inside The Downhill Skating System movements like the Anchor mechanic proves the opposite.

Relax the off leg and let your inside edge do the work, and you’ll be moving more efficient and skating with less effort.

This is just one piece of the Downhill Skating System where we teach that exact fundamentals with lessons, drills and video to skate more like McDavid, Barzal and MacKinnon, the games best skaters.

Inside Train 2.0+, you’ll get the full system, plus 10+ courses on the mechanics behind today’s best players.

The #1 Mineral to Reduce Swollen Legs and Ankles If your ankles or legs are swelling, your body may be lacking one essential mineral that plays a major role in circulation and fluid balance. In this video, I explain how magnesium helps relax blood vessels, improve circulation, and support the movement of excess fluid out of your lower body. You’ll also learn how magnesium works with potassium and sodium to regulate fluid inside your cells, why low levels can lead to swelling, and what simple steps you can take today to support your body naturally. I’ll go over how much magnesium to take, the best time to take it, the most effective forms, and easy movements that help activate your body’s natural “pump” to reduce fluid buildup in your legs and ankles.

videos.title: Nuggets FORCE Game 6,  But I’ve Got PROBLEMS | GS MoodSwings
videos.summary_source_text:
Nuggets FORCE Game 6,  But I’ve Got PROBLEMS | GS MoodSwings Welcome back to MoodSwings presented by Landmark Lincoln…your new favorite sports therapy show.

The Denver Nuggets force a Game 6 after a dominant Game 5 win over the Timberwolves — but there’s still a lot to unpack.

From Spencer Jones stepping up in a huge way, to serious concerns about depth, fatigue, and coaching decisions… this series is far from over.

Plus — thoughts on injuries, fan reactions, and why this Nuggets team still hasn’t fully convinced me.

Game 6 in Minnesota is do-or-die. Let’s talk about it.

videos.title: Penguins Stay Alive + Bruins and Oilers Look to Avoid Elimination ft. Pierre McGuire | The Sheet
videos.summary_source_text:
Penguins Stay Alive + Bruins and Oilers Look to Avoid Elimination ft. Pierre McGuire | The Sheet Jeff Marek is joined by Pierre McGuire for a loaded playoff edition of The Sheet as the Stanley Cup Playoffs continue to deliver drama across the league. They kick things off with Sidney Crosby leading the Pittsburgh Penguins to a crucial Game 5 win to stay alive against the Philadelphia Flyers, breaking down what it means for the series now sitting at 3-2. Then, they dive into the wild back-and-forth battle between the Mammoth and the Vegas Golden Knights, capped by a dramatic overtime winner to even that series. Plus, a huge night ahead as the Edmonton Oilers and Boston Bruins both fight to keep their seasons alive — Marek and McGuire preview the key storylines and pressure points heading into those elimination games. They also take a spin around the league with updates, insights, and analysis from every series as the playoff picture continues to shift. Don’t miss this full breakdown of everything happening across the Stanley Cup Playoffs.

Still using ChatGPT or stitching together 4 different tools for your systematic literature review? There's a better way.

In this video, I walk you through SciSpace's new SLR Agent — a fully customisable, PRISMA-compliant systematic literature review tool that screened 3,000+ papers in under 60 minutes. I tested it head-to-head against ChatGPT and dedicated review tools like Rayyan, Covidence, and Elicit, and the results genuinely surprised me.

I also break down SciSpace's new Skills feature — including Clinical Trial Search, Grant Research, Web Search, and Presentation Generation — that turn this from a literature review tool into a full research operating system.

What's Next For The Toronto Maple Leafs? 2026 Off-Season Plan What's Next For The Toronto Maple Leafs? 2026 Off-Season Plan
How will The Toronto Maple Leafs attempt at moving forward and making their team more competitive in 2026-27? How can they bounce back and move back into a playoffs spot after not making the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Playoffs. I examine the past season, team roster and determine their best plan to improve the team.  We address NHL Trade Rumours regarding Toronto Maple Leafs trade rumours and how they should proceed on that.  We analyze their 2026 NHL Prospects and deals they may try to make at the draft.  They are in good shape with draft picks in the 2026 NHL Draft.  We discuss the 2026 NHL Draft prospects they could select as well. There have been plenty of NHL Trade rumours 2026 regarding this team Toronto Maple Leafs rumours and whether they stand a good chance at making some off-season moves. videos.title: Get More Speed | Part 2 | Padraig Harrington
videos.summary_source_text:
Get More Speed | Part 2 | Padraig Harrington As part of my YouTube series  “Ten lessons every golfer should know”, the third Paddy’s Golf Tip covers “Speed”, which is a two-part video. 
 
In this second instalment, I explain why a short swing and stopping at impact in practise and playing with a normal length swing on the course works for me and also why releasing the arms, hands and the club is vital in order to maximise swingspeed.  I demonstrate other techniques including moving the feet at address and explain my use of carbon fibre shoe insoles and a training aid.

#PadraigHarrington #golfing #golftips #paddysgolftips #golf #feedback #vktrygolf #superspeedgolf

SUBSCRIBE FOR MORE CONTENT: https://bit.ly/4gKoVme

About Padraig Harrington: 

Born in Dublin, Padraig is a proud family man married to Caroline with two children, Patrick and Ciaran. As a three time major champion, Padraig is one of the world’s top golfers and his playing record speaks for itself. Following a very successful amateur career, including winning the Walker Cup in 1995 at Royal Porthcawl in one of his three appearances, he turned professional in September 1995.

videos.title: Playoff Recap & Leaking Oilers ft. James van Riemsdyk, Tyler Yaremchuk & Gary Lawless | MCH
videos.summary_source_text:
Playoff Recap & Leaking Oilers ft. James van Riemsdyk, Tyler Yaremchuk & Gary Lawless | MCH Season 3 rolls on with another loaded edition of Morning Cuppa Hockey as Jonny Lazarus and Colby Cohen are joined by a trio of elite hockey minds to break down everything happening across the Stanley Cup Playoffs.

First up, James van Riemsdyk drops by to break down Game 5 of the Flyers/Penguins series, and take a look through his favourite moments of the postseason thus far. 

Then, Tyler Yaremchuk stops in to preview Game 5 of the Oilers/Ducks series, and Edmonton can bounce back after a controversial Game 4 OT loss. 

Finally, Gary Lawless joins the show to recap Game 4 of the Golden Knights & Mammoth. 

Don’t miss a second of the daily hockey talk!

videos.title: 464. Finansiell rådgivning för privatpersoner med Ricard Nylund
videos.summary_source_text:
464. Finansiell rådgivning för privatpersoner med Ricard Nylund Vad är en finansiell rådgivare egentligen värd – och behöver du en?

I det här avsnittet har vi tillbaka Ricard Nylund från Ricap, mannen bakom
det numera bevingade uttrycket "Jan, när ska du släppa taget om
handbromsen?". Vi reder ut de vanligaste misstagen sparare gör på alla
förmögenhetsnivåer, och varför värdet av rådgivning sällan ligger där du
tror.

🎯 DET VANLIGASTE MISSTAGET
De tre fallgroparna Ricard ser om och om igen är överaktivitet, för låg
risk och för hög risk. Överaktivitet är det dyraste — du är med på
nedgångarna men missar uppgångarna. Och två personer med samma globala
indexfond kan ligga i helt olika risknivåer beroende på resten av deras
ekonomi.

🏗️ RICARDS PROCESS – NERIFRÅN OCH UPP
Likvidbuffert för det oväntade. Reservbuffert i lågriskplaceringar så att
livet inte sätts på paus när börsen dyker. Och sist potentialen — motorn
som ska få ligga ifred och växa. Ordningen är inte förhandlingsbar.

🏠 DEN RIKE FATTIGPENSIONÄREN
Kanske avsnittets mest konträra poäng: skuldfrihet är inte samma sak som
ekonomisk frihet. Vi pratar om varför "mest belånad vinner" — givet att
grunden är stabil — och varför ett amorteringsrace ibland kostar dig den
frihet du faktiskt sparat ihop till.

👥 FYRA TYPFALL
Anna och Martin (45, barnfamilj). Emma (30, singel, 50 000 kr sparat).
Sara (samma utgångspunkt men driver eget). Joakim (10 miljoner i
investerbart kapital). Plus den provocerande observationen att de sämsta
portföljerna Ricard ser ofta finns hos personer med 30–80 miljoner —
inte hos nybörjarna.

❓ ERA FRÅGOR
I del två svarar vi på det ni skickat in: amortera eller investera, sälja
aktier som gått dåligt, skatteplanera som en miljardär, vad göra med
plötsliga arvspengar, hitta en bra rådgivare, och vad FIRE-rörelsen ofta
missar.

✨ EN GENOMGÅENDE TRÅD
Ricard är förmodligen Sveriges tråkigaste finansiella rådgivare — och det
säger han själv. Hans mål är inte att slå index. Hans mål är att du ska
må bra av dina pengar. Och det är det här avsnittet handlar om.

Många hälsningar,
Jan, Caroline och Ricard

Finansiell rådgivning med Ricard
====
Eftersom Ricard driver eget har han inte obegränsat med tid och möjligheter. Därav att jag skriver detta för att vara schysst både mot honom och dig. 

Eftersom varje rådgivning enligt lag kräver en mycket arbete, främst i framtagande av rådgivningsdokumentation, blir den procentuella kostnaden oskäligt hög om du inte har ett investerbart kapital på över 500.000 kr exkl. pension, alternativt har icke-kollektivavtalsanslutna tjänstepensionstillgångar runt 2.000.000 kr eller driver ett AB.

Enklast är att boka tid med Ricard via denna Calendly-länk: https://calendly.com/ricap/radgivning-rikatillsammans
Hans mejl och mobiltelefon hittar du på hans hemsida: https://ricap.se/kontakt/

Länkar
=====
Så hittar du en bra finansiell rådgivare – tips, frågor och forskning: https://rikatillsammans.se/spara-och-investera/ekonomisk-radgivning-och-finansiella-radgivare/
Diskussion till avsnittet i forumet: https://rikatillsammans.se/forum/t/120766

Innehållsförteckning
======
00:00:00 - Intro till avsnittet
00:03:39 - Tre vanligaste misstagen: överaktivitet, för lite risk och för mycket risk
00:08:09 - De flesta saknar en plan för vad pengarna ska användas till
00:11:00 - En finansiell rådgivare trollar inte fram bättre avkastning
00:12:39 - Resonemang om belåning när grunden är stabil
00:14:09 - Planera för ett långt liv, inte ett slutdatum 
00:15:00 - Den bästa finansiella planen är den du faktiskt kan hålla
00:18:45 - Drömmar skjuts ofta upp för länge och hinner aldrig bli verklighet
00:19:27 - FIRE - hiss eller diss?
00:22:45 - Ricards syn på buffert
00:28:33 - Scenario: Barnfamiljen runt 45 år med lön, barn hemma och belånat boende
00:32:18 - Scenario: Ung singel på 30 år med 50 000 kr sparade – vad är viktigast nu?
00:34:00 - Scenario: Företagaren i uppstartsfas

The #1 Most Dangerous Fat For Your Body Many people consume these oils daily without realizing the potential long-term effects. If you care about your health, energy levels, and inflammation, this is something you need to understand.

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements.

👉 Get Dr. Berg’s Daily Routine as a free download: https://drbrg.co/4vWO9Ww

The most dangerous fat you can put in your body isn’t butter, egg yolks, tallow, or even coconut oil. It’s something far more common: industrial seed oils like corn oil, soybean oil, canola oil, and cottonseed oil.

These oils are found in almost every restaurant meal, salad dressing, and processed food. They’re marketed as “heart-healthy,” but the truth is very different.

👋 WELCOME — I’M RICK SHIELS

This channel is all about helping you play better golf and enjoy the game more.
I make videos for every type of golfer — beginners, weekend players, improving golfers and low handicaps chasing new personal bests.

Here’s what you’ll find on the channel:

⭐ GOLF COACHING & LESSONS

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook
• Hit your driver longer and straighter
• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance
• Learn how to draw and fade the golf ball
• Improve ball position, alignment and setup
• Build a repeatable, consistent golf swing
• Increase clubhead speed
• Improve swing path and club face control

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

⭐ GOLF EQUIPMENT REVIEWS (H.I.T)

I test the latest:

• Drivers
• Irons
• Putters
• Wedges
• Golf balls
• Launch monitors
• Golf gadgets
• Budget vs premium clubs

All reviews are completely honest and independent. I use Trackman and GCQuad to give you accurate numbers you can trust.

If you’re trying to find the best golf driver, best irons, most forgiving clubs or value golf equipment — you’re in the right place.

⭐ BREAK 75 & GOLF ENTERTAINMENT

Break 75 has become one of YouTube’s biggest golf series.
You’ll see:

• Full 18-hole course vlogs
• Course management strategies
• Matches against tour players and creators
• Pressure moments, birdie runs and disaster holes
• Some of the world’s best golf courses
• Real, relatable golf

I also play matches and challenges with some of the biggest names in golf, including:

• Bryson DeChambeau
• Peter Finch
• Good Good
• GM Golf
• Bob Does Sports
• BustaJack
• Grant Horvat
• Me and My Golf
• Danny Maude

These help bring you the most entertaining and competitive golf content possible.

⭐ TRAVEL, COURSE VLOGS & LIV CONTENT

I film at some of the most incredible golf destinations around the world, including LIV tournament venues.
Expect:

• Amazing golf courses
• Behind-the-scenes access
• Player interactions
• Travel insights
• Bucket-list destinations
• Worldwide golf adventures

⭐ THE GOAL

To make golf more fun, more accessible and help you become a better, more confident, happier golfer — whether you’re breaking 100 or trying to break par. Be sure to subscribe to LIV Golf here:
https://www.youtube.com/@LIVGolf

videos.title: SHINE ON! Carolina Hurricanes' 3 Stars of Round 1 Against the Ottawa Senators
videos.summary_source_text:
SHINE ON! Carolina Hurricanes' 3 Stars of Round 1 Against the Ottawa Senators While the Carolina Hurricanes wait on who they'll face in Round 2, let's look back on Round 1. When taking a look again at Round 1, who are the three stars against the Ottawa Senators?  Was it a whole line? Was it goaltending? Depth maybe? On the Tuesday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach starts the show by sharing his thoughts on his combined 3rd Star of Logan Stankoven and Jackson Blake. The two young players played well throughout the series. Could one of them be moved higher? Could someone else have made the three-star list? Why does it make sense to combine them? Secondly, Zach shares his thoughts on why Frederik Andersen is the 2nd Star of the series. Is there an argument to move Andersen to the 1st Star after his phenomenal Round 1? Will he keep this pace going? Finally, Zach concludes the show by sharing why Taylor Hall just edges out Andersen for the 1st Star. Could there be a case to be had on Hall, maybe being just behind Andersen for the top spot? All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

videos.title: This BMW has a BIG PROBLEM
videos.summary_source_text:
This BMW has a BIG PROBLEM Explore new and used cars with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-2804

It’s time for three of the most sought-after electric SUVs to go head-to-head to see which deserves a spot on your driveway. 

In one corner, we have the global best-seller - and recently updated - Tesla Model Y. It is being challenged by a fresh, high-tech rival from Ingolstadt, the Audi Q6 Sportback e-tron, and the latest all-electric offering from Munich, the BMW iX3. 

These three represent the cutting edge of premium family transport, but they each take a very different approach to design, performance, and daily usability.

The Tesla Model Y AWD Long Range remains the benchmark that everyone else is trying to beat. Known for its class-leading efficiency and unparalleled charging infrastructure, the Model Y offers a dual-motor setup that provides sure-footed all-wheel-drive traction and a 0-60mph time that puts many sports cars to shame. 

Inside, it’s as minimalist as every car in Tesla’s line-up, dominated by a single massive touchscreen that controls almost every function of the car. While some may find the lack of physical buttons polarising, there is no denying the sheer amount of interior space and the clever storage solutions, including a massive "frunk" and a cavernous rear boot.

Moving to the Audi Q6 Sportback RWD Performance, we see a focus on sleek aesthetics and long-distance touring. By opting for the rear-wheel-drive "Performance" variant, Audi has prioritised range, utilising a large battery and a more aerodynamic Sportback silhouette to extract maximum miles from every charge. 

Built on the new PPE platform shared with Porsche, the Q6 features an ultra-fast 800V charging architecture, allowing you to add hundreds of miles in the time it takes to grab a coffee. The interior features Audi’s new "Digital Stage" OLED display layout, although we’re not a fan of the huge bezels around the screen! Thankfully, though, there are high-quality materials throughout. 

The BMW iX3 50 xDrive represents the latest evolution of BMW's electric SUV lineup, now boasting a dual-motor configuration for significantly more punch than its predecessor. BMW has focused heavily on the driving experience, ensuring that even as a heavy SUV, it retains that signature "Ultimate Driving Machine" DNA with sharp steering and composed handling. 

It also features a massive battery capacity that pushes its range toward the 500-mile mark in ideal conditions, and like the Audi, it utilises an 800V system for lightning-fast top-ups. The cabin features the all-new panoramic iVision display, which, while impressive, potentially offers a lot of information that isn’t that useful on a trip to the shops! 

This group test isn't just about the numbers on a spec sheet; it’s about how these cars handle the real world. Mat will be putting all three through their paces, testing everything from their real-world efficiency and interior quality to their infotainment systems and rear-seat practicality. By the end of this deep dive, we’ll see if the Tesla can hold onto its crown or if the German giants have finally caught up to - and overtaken - the most popular electric SUV of recent years!

Chapters
00:00 Audi Q6 vs Tesla Model Y vs BMW iX3
00:12 Motor & 0-60 Performance Specs
00:34 EV Drag Race: Audi vs Tesla vs BMW
02:08 Rolling Race & Passing Power
03:33 60-0mph Emergency Brake Test
04:07 Range & 800V Charging Tech

12:50 Boot Space & Practicality
16:17 Front Boot (Frunk) Comparison
17:31 UK Price & Value Analysis

videos.title: Intermittent Fasting Doubles Your Risk of Dying from a Heart Attack
videos.summary_source_text:
Intermittent Fasting Doubles Your Risk of Dying from a Heart Attack Recent news reports have claimed that intermittent fasting can lead to cardiovascular death. What’s the truth?

DATA:
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10115489/
https://link.springer.com/article/10.1186/s12967-020-02687-0
https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fnut.2021.669325/full 
https://www.jacc.org/doi/full/10.1016/j.jaccao.2022.03.002

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

👉 Get Dr. Berg’s Daily Routine as a free download: https://drbrg.co/4vKsp01

0:00 Introduction: Does fasting cause cardiovascular problems?
1:09 Poor data on intermittent fasting
2:36 Peer-reviewed studies supporting intermittent fasting 
4:26 Fasting does not increase cardiovascular risk

Today, we’re going to talk about some of the recent news headlines claiming that fasting can wreak havoc on your cardiovascular system. This information was taken from an abstract (short summary) of an observational study sponsored by the American Heart Association.

The study was based on a 24-hour dietary recall, where participants had to recall everything they ate over a period of time. The author has also conducted observational studies that directly opposed red meat, eggs, and cholesterol. 

Credible, peer-reviewed studies have shown numerous benefits of intermittent fasting and show that intermittent fasting actually supports cardiovascular health. 

1. A single-blind randomized study examined the long-term effects of time-restricted eating with resistance training over 12 months. Participants saw a significant decrease in body mass, fat mass, and inflammation.

They experienced increased insulin sensitivity and a more balanced lipid profile without adverse effects. Intermittent fasting was deemed safe and effective for improving cardiovascular function. 

2. A non-randomized controlled clinical trial for time-restricted feeding and its effect on body composition and cardiovascular risk found that 18 hours of fasting significantly decreased waist circumference and cardiovascular risk. 

3. A systematic review of intermittent fasting with cardiometabolic risk factors looked at four randomized controlled trials. The results demonstrated that intermittent fasting significantly reduced body weight, waist circumference, body mass, fat mass, blood pressure, cholesterol, triglycerides, and fasting insulin levels.

4. One study evaluated a 16-hour fasting eating pattern with an 8-hour eating window on breast cancer survivors for 8 weeks. Results demonstrated a significant decrease in cardiovascular risk. 

Dr. Eric Berg DC Bio:

0:00 Introduction: Does fasting cause cardiovascular problems?
1:09 Poor data on intermittent fasting
2:36 Peer-reviewed studies supporting intermittent fasting 
4:26 Fasting does not increase cardiovascular risk

Today, we’re going to talk about some of the recent news headlines claiming that fasting can wreak havoc on your cardiovascular system. This information was taken from an abstract (short summary) of an observational study sponsored by the American Heart Association.

The study was based on a 24-hour dietary recall, where participants had to recall everything they ate over a period of time. The author has also conducted observational studies that directly opposed red meat, eggs, and cholesterol. 

Credible, peer-reviewed studies have shown numerous benefits of intermittent fasting and show that intermittent fasting actually supports cardiovascular health. 

1. A single-blind randomized study examined the long-term effects of time-restricted eating with resistance training over 12 months. Participants saw a significant decrease in body mass, fat mass, and inflammation.

They experienced increased insulin sensitivity and a more balanced lipid profile without adverse effects. Intermittent fasting was deemed safe and effective for improving cardiovascular function. 

2. A non-randomized controlled clinical trial for time-restricted feeding and its effect on body composition and cardiovascular risk found that 18 hours of fasting significantly decreased waist circumference and cardiovascular risk. 

3. A systematic review of intermittent fasting with cardiometabolic risk factors looked at four randomized controlled trials. The results demonstrated that intermittent fasting significantly reduced body weight, waist circumference, body mass, fat mass, blood pressure, cholesterol, triglycerides, and fasting insulin levels.

4. One study evaluated a 16-hour fasting eating pattern with an 8-hour eating window on breast cancer survivors for 8 weeks. Results demonstrated a significant decrease in cardiovascular risk.

videos.summary_source_text:
Jokic, Nuggets Strike Back in Game 5 | DNVR Nuggets Postgame Live The Nuggets are still alive after getting a massive blowout win at home. The Timberwolves didn’t have nearly as much fight this time around without Anthony Edwards and Donte DiVencenzo. The Spencer Jones game! Nikola Jokic had another 3rd quarter triple double, and the Nuggets offense was flowing free for the first time this series. The Nuggets now have to go into Minnesota on Thursday night and steal a win on the road to send the series to a game 7. videos.title: UK | 27th April, Monday - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
UK | 27th April, Monday - RIFT Presentation 27/04/26

🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

You don’t need to be a professional trader or understand complex charts. RIFT does the heavy lifting, helping you participate in markets with speed, structure, and efficiency.

📊 Built for the modern era of trading, where automation and algorithmic execution dominate over manual trading.

⚠️ Disclaimer: Trading involves risk. Always understand fees, leverage, and market conditions before trading. 27/04/26

videos.title: MULTIPLE CALLS: Söderblom BURIES First Career Playoff Goal! 🇸🇪 🔥
videos.summary_source_text:
MULTIPLE CALLS: Söderblom BURIES First Career Playoff Goal! 🇸🇪 🔥 Elmer Söderblom collects a feed from Anthony Mantha below the goal line and blasts it home with the Pittsburgh Penguins first shot on goal, giving them an early 1-0 lead. It marks Söderblom’s first career playoff goal as Pittsburgh goes on to beat the Philadelphia Flyers 3-2 in Game 5 to stay alive in the series. Relive the moment with the Penguins, ESPN, and Flyers broadcasts.

videos.title: OpenAI Is Building The AI Phone Apple Should Fear
videos.summary_source_text:
OpenAI Is Building The AI Phone Apple Should Fear 👉 Try GPT Image 2 + Seedance 2.0 here: https://higgsfield.ai/s/gpt-image-2-seedance-2-0-airevolutionx-FAepGl
OpenAI may be preparing for something bigger than another chatbot. New reports say OpenAI is working with MediaTek, Qualcomm, and Luxshare on an AI-native phone that could turn ChatGPT from an app into the main brain of the device.

📩 Brand Deals & Partnerships: collabs@nouralabs.com
✉ General Inquiries: airevolutionofficial@gmail.com
🚀 New Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@science.revolution

🧠 What You’ll See
0:00 Intro
0:21 OpenAI reportedly works with MediaTek and Qualcomm on AI phone processors
SOURCE: https://www.reuters.com/world/china/qualcomm-surges-report-openai-tie-up-ai-smartphone-processors-2026-04-27/
0:58 Why AI Agents Need Hardware Control
2:57 Cloud + On-Device AI Architecture
3:34 The 300 Million Phone Market Bet
4:34 OpenAI’s hardware push includes Jony Ive, LoveFrom, and new AI devices
SOURCE: https://www.theinformation.com/articles/inside-openai-team-developing-ai-devices
5:06 The Full Product Lineup (Speaker, Headphones, Glasses)
5:47 Altman's "Lakeside Cottage" Vision
7:24 China's Doubao Phone & The Charter Rewrite
8:02 Doubao's GUI Agent Approach
8:31 Why WeChat & Alipay Blocked Doubao
8:59 ByteDance and ZTE push the Doubao AI phone in China
SOURCE: https://www.scmp.com/news/china-future-tech/ai/article/3334911/bytedance-powered-ai-phone-sparks-online-rush-consumers-keen-try-agentic-assistant
10:05 Two Paths, Same Conclusion
10:26 Altman's Contradictory AGI Posts
10:58 The AGI Skeptics Speak Up
11:26 OpenAI's Quiet Charter Rewrite

🚨 Why It Matters
AI agents may need more than apps to reach their full potential. If OpenAI controls the phone, the chip, and the software layer, ChatGPT could become the main interface for your daily tasks, apps, payments, messages, and routines.

videos.title: Mathew Barzal Wide Forward Stride
videos.summary_source_text:
Mathew Barzal Wide Forward Stride The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

videos.title: Beer League Hockey Workout 🏒
videos.summary_source_text:
Beer League Hockey Workout 🏒 You’re not going to the NHL from beer leagues but if you’re already working out or have goals to get in shape, why not train for hockey performance?

Beer league is just for fun. But it’s a lot more fun when you aren’t gasping for air on the bench, legs don’t feel like lead, and you actually have the explosive power to blow past players.

Our newest Men’s League Domination ‘26 has just launched with a new Speed Accelerator system and 40+ new exercises, all on our new app Hockey Training HQ available on iOS and Android. videos.title: Connor McDavid’s Counterintuitive Attack Pattern
videos.summary_source_text:
Connor McDavid’s Counterintuitive Attack Pattern In this video, we’re diving into Connor McDavid’s drive wide guide. Whether you’re a fan of hockey superstar Connor McDavid or simply aiming to up your game, we’ve got an exciting formula to share with you.

You might be wondering, what’s the secret behind McDavid’s net driving success? Hint: it’s not all about his speed. There’s a strategic approach that sets him apart. And the best part? You don’t need to be the fastest skater out there to master this technique. 

Let’s break it down step by step. McDavid’s winning formula begins when he gains possession of the puck between the dots, positioning himself strategically within this zone. Step two involves a crucial move which is a crossover or weight shift towards the boards. This creates space between him and the defenseman, a key advantage that you’ll witness in action.

But here comes the game-changer: step three. McDavid initiates crossing over towards the defenseman. This isn’t just about speed; it’s about efficiency and deception. Crossing over proves to be not only faster than regular forward skating but also incredibly deceptive, giving him the edge he needs.

Contrast this with the typical approaches other players take. They might rush straight down from the boards or head directly into the corner, missing out on the strategy that makes McDavid’s drives so effective. His pattern starts between the dots, widens out, and he smartly crosses over towards the defenseman, giving him the upper hand every time. videos.title: The Ducks Win Has the Hockey World Divided — And Tkachuk Watch Intensifies | DFO Rundown
videos.summary_source_text:
The Ducks Win Has the Hockey World Divided — And Tkachuk Watch Intensifies | DFO Rundown Welcome to the DFO Rundown with Jason Gregor and Jeff Marek — your daily breakdown of the Stanley Cup Playoffs.

We kick things off with the Tampa Bay Lightning vs Montreal Canadiens, where Brandon Hagel steps up in a huge way as Tampa continues to rely on its core in a tightly contested series.

Then we dive into the biggest storyline of the night — the controversial overtime finish between the Edmonton Oilers and Anaheim Ducks. Did the NHL get the call right? We break down the OT winner, the review process, and growing injury concerns surrounding Edmonton as the series heats up.

We also get into:
🔥 Buy or Sell powered by Bet365
📈 Buffalo Sabres taking control vs Boston Bruins

videos.title: Do This for 30 Seconds — You’ll Feel It Instantly
videos.summary_source_text:
Do This for 30 Seconds — You’ll Feel It Instantly Pressing into your ribs for just 30 seconds can immediately change how your body feels. This simple technique helps activate your nervous system, improve breathing, and bring your body into a calmer, more relaxed state.

As you apply gentle pressure to your rib cage, you stimulate intercostal nerves and influence the diaphragm, which connects directly to the vagus nerve. This can slow your heart rate, reduce stress, and help your body shift into that powerful “rest and recover” mode.

Most people feel it right away — a deeper breath, a lighter chest, and a sense of calm spreading through the body.

Try it right now for 30 seconds… then come back and tell me what you felt.

📌 PubMed Research for Reference:

Vagus nerve stimulation and stress reduction: PMID: 32198494
Diaphragmatic breathing and sleep improvement: PMID: 29535284
Autonomic nervous system regulation through respiratory mechanics: PMID: 29947685

videos.title: Claude Mythos: The AI Anthropic Refuses to Release (And Why It Matters)
videos.summary_source_text:
Claude Mythos: The AI Anthropic Refuses to Release (And Why It Matters) Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Anthropic just built Claude Mythos Preview — a frontier AI model that found thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities in seven weeks, including flaws in every major operating system and browser. Then they refused to release it.

In this video, I break down what Claude Mythos actually is, what it can really do (with the actual benchmark numbers), the messy Capybara leak that forced Anthropic's hand, and the four real reasons behind the lockdown — ranked by how likely each one is.

We cover Project Glasswing, the AISI evaluation, the unauthorized-access incident, and what this all means for developers, security pros, and anyone who builds software online. Plus the skeptical take: is this a genuine watershed moment, or is Sam Altman right that it's "fear-based marketing"?

The setup mistakes that affect your contact
How your swing path influences strike consistency
Why over-controlling your technique makes things worse
How to blend technique with feel for better results

Ducks Push Oilers to the Brink and Bolts Even Series with Habs ft. Greg Wyshynski | The Sheet Jeff Marek is joined by Greg Wyshynski for another edition of MvsW on The Sheet as they break down a wild night across the Stanley Cup Playoffs, headlined by the Anaheim Ducks taking a shocking 3-1 series lead over the Edmonton Oilers following a controversial overtime winner in Game 4 that has everyone talking. Marek and Wysh dive into the fallout for Connor McDavid and the Oilers, what went wrong, and whether there’s any path back in the series. They also discuss the Tampa Bay Lightning evening things up against the Montreal Canadiens as the series shifts back to Florida, plus a look at two sweeps with the LA Kings and Ottawa Senators both getting eliminated. It’s a full playoff vibe check with storylines, pressure points, and what to watch next. Subscribe for more daily NHL coverage on The Sheet.

videos.title: DeepSeek V4 Explained: 1.6 Trillion Parameters & 1 Million Token Context
videos.summary_source_text:
DeepSeek V4 Explained: 1.6 Trillion Parameters & 1 Million Token Context Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
DeepSeek V4 just dropped — 1.6 trillion parameters, 1 million token context, fully open source. In this video, I break down exactly what makes V4 different, how the architecture actually works, and whether it can really compete with GPT-5.4, Claude, and Gemini 3.

🔍 WHAT'S COVERED:
00:00 - Intro
01:31 - Who Is DeepSeek and Why Should You Care?
02:59 - The Key Features That Make V4 Different
05:17 - Technical Deep Dive
07:52 - V4 vs. The Frontier: GPT-4o, Llama 3, Claude 3, Gemini 3
10:20 - A Real Demo: What V4 Actually Does
11:48 - Practical Use Cases You Can Actually Run Today
13:26 - The Limitations You Need to Know About

⚡ KEY SPECS:
- V4-Pro: 1.6T total parameters, 49B active per token
- V4-Flash: 284B total, 13B active
- 1,000,000 token context window (default)
- MIT licensed — fully open weights
- V4-Flash: $0.14/M input, $0.28/M output

🧠 ARCHITECTURE HIGHLIGHTS:
✅ Mixture-of-Experts with 384 distributed experts
✅ Compressed Sparse Attention + Heavily Compressed Attention
✅ Manifold-Constrained Hyper-Connections
✅ Trained with the new Muon optimizer
✅ FP4 precision on expert weights

🆚 HOW IT COMPARES:
- Largest open-weight LLM ever released
- Matches GPT-5.4 on coding benchmarks
- ~4x cheaper than Claude 3.5 Haiku on output tokens
- Ties GPT-4o for longest context window
- Text-only (no vision or audio)

💼 BEST USE CASES:
- Long-document Q&A (legal, research, finance)
- Full codebase analysis & agentic coding
- High-volume conversational AI
- Multilingual applications
- Custom fine-tuned deployments


📌 RELATED VIDEOS:
- GPT-5.5 Full Review: https://youtu.be/5iQcpXSpv2c 
- Claude vs Chatgpt Comparison: https://youtu.be/Om4bU0iaLAc

💬 Question: Would you actually deploy V4 in production, or is the closed-model ecosystem still worth the premium? Let me know in the comments.

👍 If this helped, hit the like button — it genuinely makes a difference.
🔔 Subscribe for more honest AI deep-dives.

videos.title: How to Check if AI is Recommending Your Business (Free Tool)
videos.summary_source_text:
How to Check if AI is Recommending Your Business (Free Tool) *Start a free trial of HubSpot's new AEO tool:* https://clickhubspot.com/8g0k

I walk through how I use HubSpot’s AEO tool to check brand visibility in AI search.
AI search is changing how people find brands. More people now ask ChatGPT, Gemini, and Perplexity for advice, ideas, and product choices. That means brands need to know how they show up in AI answer engines.

I show how answer engine optimization, or AEO, works inside HubSpot. I go through the free trial setup, add a brand, pick competitors, choose an ideal customer, and review the prompts HubSpot tracks.

Then I look at the AEO dashboard. I check brand visibility, brand sentiment, share of voice, prompt results, citations, and content recommendations. This helps me see how my brand shows up in ChatGPT and other AI tools.
I also show how HubSpot gives clear next steps, like what content to make and which citations matter. This can help improve AI visibility, AEO results, and how often your brand shows up in AI answers.

If you want to understand AEO, AI search, HubSpot’s AEO tool, ChatGPT visibility, prompt tracking, citations, and brand visibility in AI answer engines, this walkthrough will help. *Start a free trial of HubSpot's new AEO tool:* https://clickhubspot.com/8g0k

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Hi I’m Matt Fryer, welcome to my channel MattFryerGolf. This YouTube channel is to help you play better golf and love the game even more!

I specialise in golf lessons, golf club news and other entertaining golf content.

I you want to play better golf I cover all topics in my golf lessons such as, how to fix your slice, fix your hook, help you drive the golf ball longer, hit your irons consistently , master your shortgame and hole more putts. 
I will also help you lower your score by giving you plans and tips on managing your game out on the golf course, with course vlogs and golf course scenarios, so you know exactly what to do you find yourself in a tricky situation.  

On this channel I also make fun and entertaining golf content from golfing trips I go on, showcasing some of the best golf courses and resorts in the world and giving you advice on where to visit and which golf courses to play.

This channel is all about helping you play better golf and enjoy the game more.
I make videos for every type of golfer — beginners, weekend players, improving golfers and low handicaps chasing new personal bests.

Here’s what you’ll find on the channel:

⭐ GOLF COACHING & LESSONS

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook
• Hit your driver longer and straighter
• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance
• Learn how to draw and fade the golf ball
• Improve ball position, alignment and setup
• Build a repeatable, consistent golf swing
• Increase clubhead speed
• Improve swing path and club face control

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

⭐ GOLF EQUIPMENT REVIEWS (H.I.T)

I test the latest:

• Drivers

All reviews are completely honest and independent. I use Trackman and GCQuad to give you accurate numbers you can trust.

• Amazing golf courses

videos.title: I Tested Golf’s Biggest Myth…The Results Surprised Me!
videos.summary_source_text:
I Tested Golf’s Biggest Myth…The Results Surprised Me! In this episode, we finally put the ultimate debate to the test: Would you score lower if a Pro hit all your drives, or if a Pro hit all your approach shots? Most golfers are told that "it's all about the short game," but the data often tells a different story. We took 10-handicapper Rory Anderson out on the course to experiment with both scenarios. We analyse everything from penalty strokes off the tee to proximity to the hole on approach shots to see which professional intervention actually saves the most strokes for the average player.

What You’ll Learn:
The Impact of Distance: How a pro-level drive changes your club selection for the rest of the hole.

Penalty Avoidance: Why the driver might be the most "destructive" club for an amateur.

Proximity to Hole: Does a pro's iron play actually lead to more birdies for a 10 handicap?

Course Management: Why knowing your "miss" is the secret to breaking 80.

Chapters:

00:00 Introduction
00:30 Hole 1 (Driving)
02:22 Hole 2 (Driving)
06:06 Hole 3 (Driving)
10:02 Hole 4 (Driving)
12:19 Hole 5 (Approach)
16:04 Hole 6 (Approach)
19:51 Hole 7 (Approach)
22:49 Final Hole (Approach)
27:56 My Verdict

videos.title: The BEST putter L.A.B have EVER made 👀
videos.summary_source_text:
The BEST putter L.A.B have EVER made 👀 Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

Why H.I.T exists:
To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.
To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.

videos.summary_source_text:
McLaren's 'Best Chance'? | Miami GP Preview | F1 Nation Podcast Can a 'completely new' McLaren take the victory in Miami? Tom Clarkson, James Hinchcliffe and Jolyon Palmer think it's the papaya team's best chance of the season so far. F1 is back after a break from racing, and everything could be different!

Tom, Jolyon and Hinch debate which team will make the biggest leap forward in Miami, and how the second F1 Sprint weekend of the year puts pressure on drivers to get back up to speed quickly. Can McLaren get ahead of Mercedes? Have Ferrari got special upgrades? Will Red Bull rise back to the front?

Plus, the team celebrate the news that F1 is returning to Istanbul Park - a 'modern classic' which always produces exciting racing. We're already looking forward to the 2027 Turkish Grand Prix...

videos.title: Why You Are So TIRED (It's This Deficiency)
videos.summary_source_text:
Why You Are So TIRED (It's This Deficiency) There are many possible reasons why you feel tired all the time. It’s important to identify and address the root cause, not the symptom. Find out how to boost energy naturally by addressing the most common chronic fatigue causes at the source. 

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements.

0:00 Introduction: Why am I always tired? 
0:23 Constant fatigue causes 
1:22 Fatigue and blood sugar
4:26 Latent virus fatigue
6:56 Low oxygen and chronic fatigue
9:13 Low vitamin B1 and fatigue symptoms
13:30 Adrenal fatigue

Keep watching for more energy-boosting tips and more ways to improve your sleep:
▶️ https://youtu.be/I2GApkHYVIo
▶️ https://youtu.be/pJ9xTMkGxXg
▶️ https://youtu.be/gGuF6X1cd_Y

Mold video: 
https://youtu.be/ngE3q-fnz1I

Healthy Ketogenic Diet and IF: 
https://youtu.be/vMZfyEy_jpI

Download Dr. Berg's Free Daily Health Routine: https://drbrg.co/4txQLZm

Stimulating the body with caffeine can actually make fatigue worse. Instead of masking the problem, you need to get to the root cause of fatigue. Once you address it, you can often get rid of chronic fatigue completely.

Not getting enough sleep significantly affects cognitive function. It also impacts blood sugar, increasing cravings and leading to more snacking. If you have insomnia, focus on simple habits like taking long walks in nature during the day without your cell phone, stretching before bed, dimming the lights at night, and avoiding snacking before sleep.

If your blood sugar is constantly going up and down, you’re going to feel tired. Even if there’s plenty of food available, your cells aren’t getting the fuel they need. One of the key solutions is to eliminate sugar and excess carbs from the diet.

videos.title: READ IT & SWEEP! Carolina Hurricanes Swept the Ottawa Senators Following their Game 4 Win
videos.summary_source_text:
READ IT & SWEEP! Carolina Hurricanes Swept the Ottawa Senators Following their Game 4 Win The Carolina Hurricanes swept the Ottawa Senators with a 4-2 win in Game 4 in Ottawa on Saturday night. What stood out in a chaotic game that saw the Hurricanes advance to Round 2? On the Monday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach starts the show by sharing his thoughts on Game 4 as a whole. From the goals, the saves, to the chaos, and so much more. It was a game of attrition, but in the end, the Hurricanes swept the Senators with their 4-2 win. Secondly, Zach shares his thoughts on the series as a whole, especially with how the Hurricanes performed in all four games. What stood out the most, leading to the series sweep? Finally, Zach concludes the show by sharing his thoughts on who the Hurricanes could face in Round 1 between either the Pittsburgh Penguins or the Philadelphia Flyers. With them playing Game 5 on Monday night, who could take that game? All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

His previous win came 5 years ago in Farsø, Denmark where he beat the likes of Robert MacIntyre and Min Woo Lee.

videos.title: Dobes Makes Buzzer-Beating Save! 🇨🇿🚨
videos.summary_source_text:

A huge last-second save for the Montreal Canadiens to preserve a 2-1 lead heading into the third period in Game 4 of the first-round playoff series.

videos.title: Avalanche Complete Sweep of LA Kings. Will Have All Week to Rest Up for Wild or Stars
videos.summary_source_text:
Avalanche Complete Sweep of LA Kings. Will Have All Week to Rest Up for Wild or Stars Colorado Avalanche sweep the Los Angeles Kings as Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, and Scott Wedgewood shine—can the Avs sustain this playoff dominance through a long postseason run?

Hosts Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson break down the Avalanche’s commanding performance, spotlighting crucial goals from MacKinnon and Toews, defensive excellence, and Wedgewood’s steady presence in net. Discussion zeroes in on Artturi Lehkonen’s relentless forechecking, Makar’s highlight-reel skills, and the impact of lineup depth with key contributions from players like Nick Blankenberg. The crew explores the Kings’ struggles, the psychological advantage of earning extra rest, and Anze Kopitar’s possible final moments with LA. Plus, analysis of potential second-round opponents and whether extra days off will be a blessing or a curse for Colorado’s Cup hopes.

Is this Avalanche squad peaking at the perfect time, or will the layoff slow their momentum?

videos.title: PGA TOUR Highlights | Round 4 | Zurich Classic of New Orleans | 2026
videos.summary_source_text:
PGA TOUR Highlights | Round 4 | Zurich Classic of New Orleans | 2026 Check out all the best shots from final-round foursomes at the 2026 Zurich Classic of New Orleans, featuring Matt Fitzpatrick, Alex Fitzpatrick, Davis Thompson and Austin Eckroat, among others.

It’s once again time to team up at the Zurich Classic of New Orleans, the PGA TOUR’s only team event, held at TPC Louisiana. Now in its ninth year as a pairs format, the event mixes Four-ball and Foursomes across four days, delivering a unique test and plenty of volatility as duos battle for momentum and FedExCup points with the Cadillac Championship on the horizon. Fresh off his playoff win at the RBC Heritage, Matt Fitzpatrick teams up with brother Alex Fitzpatrick, forming one of the most in-form pairings in the field.  A major storyline sees Brooks Koepka return to the event alongside past champion Shane Lowry who won in 2024 with back-to-back Masters champion Rory McIlroy, while defending champions Ben Griffin and Andrew Novak look to make history as the first team to go back-to-back. Rising stars are also well represented, with Michael Brennan and Johnny Keefer making their debuts, while Blades Brown pairs with Luke Clanton as part of the next wave of talent. International pairings add further intrigue, including Tom Kim with Kevin Yu, and the South African duo of Erik van Rooyen and Christiaan Bezuidenhout. With no other event like it on TOUR, the Zurich Classic blends chemistry, strategy and shot making and with major qualification pathways and Signature Event spots in play, there’s plenty on the line in New Orleans.

It’s once again time to team up at the Zurich Classic of New Orleans, the PGA TOUR’s only team event, held at TPC Louisiana. Now in its ninth year as a pairs format, the event mixes Four-ball and Foursomes across four days, delivering a unique test and plenty of volatility as duos battle for momentum and FedExCup points with the Cadillac Championship on the horizon. Fresh off his playoff win at the RBC Heritage, Matt Fitzpatrick teams up with brother Alex Fitzpatrick, forming one of the most in-form pairings in the field.  A major storyline sees Brooks Koepka return to the event alongside past champion Shane Lowry who won in 2024 with back-to-back Masters champion Rory McIlroy, while defending champions Ben Griffin and Andrew Novak look to make history as the first team to go back-to-back. Rising stars are also well represented, with Michael Brennan and Johnny Keefer making their debuts, while Blades Brown pairs with Luke Clanton as part of the next wave of talent. International pairings add further intrigue, including Tom Kim with Kevin Yu, and the South African duo of Erik van Rooyen and Christiaan Bezuidenhout. - Launch

All huge components that impact your golf ball choices and play a MASSIVE role in the performance you can get out of your golf clubs.

In addition as the conditions of a golf course change in relation to ground conditions and the weather the void between selecting the correct ball for your game becomes greater.

I compare the premium TP5X to the TaylorMade Soft Response in this video but you can expand out to other models and use the same concepts shown here.

A golf ball fitting is crucial to your testing and IDEALLY so too is seeing the ball flight into breeze and off grass. Both those elements will exaggerate the performance of your golf ball choices.

videos.title: McDavid vs. MacKinnon Crossunder Edition
videos.summary_source_text:
McDavid vs. MacKinnon Crossunder Edition In this video we take a quick look at Nathan MacKinnon and Connor McDavid’s crossunders. 

The cadence and size of the cross can vary depending on your style, and it’s important to feel comfortable with both smaller and larger steps. The rocker is also crucial, and you want to focus on landing at the heel with good shin angle, and then pushing off into a rocker that lands at the toe. 

It’s important to stay loose throughout the movement, with flexible ankles, loose hips, and relaxed shoulders. At Train 2.0, we focus on the underfoot and making sure we have a solid rocker, as it gives more power and mechanical leverage. Don’t overthink the technique, just focus on feeling comfortable and keeping that knee over your toe. In this video we take a quick look at Nathan MacKinnon and Connor McDavid’s crossunders.

videos.title: Do This to Remove Hyperpigmentation FAST
videos.summary_source_text:
Do This to Remove Hyperpigmentation FAST Hyperpigmentation is not just a surface issue, it’s a reflection of what’s happening underneath your skin at a cellular level. In this video, you’ll learn why dark spots form, how melanocytes respond to stress, inflammation, blood sugar spikes, and oxidative damage, and why most treatments fail to deliver lasting results. Instead of chasing endless creams and quick fixes, this approach focuses on correcting the root causes while using a simple, targeted method to help fade existing pigmentation more effectively. By understanding how your skin truly works and supporting it from both the inside and outside, you give your body the ability to restore a more even, healthier appearance over time.

This simple golf setup adjustment can help you:
Hit cleaner, more consistent shots
Improve your ball striking
Eliminate common swing mistakes
Build a repeatable, confident setup

videos.title: OpenAI Is Burning $12B a Quarter — Can They Beat Google?
videos.summary_source_text:
OpenAI Is Burning $12B a Quarter — Can They Beat Google? Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
OpenAI vs Google Gemini — who's actually winning the AI race in 2026? I went deep into Alphabet's 10-K, OpenAI's leaked financials, and every regulatory filing I could find, and the truth completely flipped my view.

One company is burning $12 BILLION in a single quarter. The other made more last year than most countries' entire GDP. In this breakdown, I cover the real financials, the tech stack, distribution moats, regulatory risks, and the talent war nobody's talking about — so you can see which company is actually positioned to win.

🔍 WHAT WE COVER:
00:00 The AI race isn't what you think

02:23 OpenAI's financials — the burn rate nobody talks about

05:58 GPT vs Gemini — the tech showdown

videos.title: The BEST Meal Plan to Lose Fat Faster (EAT THIS WAY!)
videos.summary_source_text:
The BEST Meal Plan to Lose Fat Faster (EAT THIS WAY!) If nutrition keeps tripping you up… if you feel like you can train hard but can’t stay consistent with your diet… then this is the video you need to watch.

Because the truth is — you haven’t failed your diet.

Your diet has failed you.

Get the Flex Factor Meal Plan - https://athleanx.com

And I’m going to show you exactly why.

In this video, I’m breaking down one of the biggest lies in fitness — the idea that you need to follow a rigid, perfect, all-or-nothing meal plan to get lean, build muscle, and stay that way. That’s exactly why most people never make it. They fall off before they ever give themselves a real chance to succeed.

Here’s the reality:
Almost 80% of your ability to stick to a nutrition plan comes down to what happens in the FIRST 10 DAYS.

And most people never even make it there.

Why?
Because they think they have to be perfect. Perfect food choices.Perfect timing.Perfect portions. And the moment something doesn’t go exactly as planned… they quit. That’s the mistake.

Because when you actually understand how nutrition works — and how flexible it really is — everything changes.

In this video, I show you:
 • Why nutrition—not training—is the biggest driver of fat loss
 • How to set your protein intake correctly based on your goal (fat loss, muscle gain, or maintenance)
 • Why most diet plans fail due to perceived rigidity—not actual requirements
 • How to use a simple plate division method to build meals that work anywhere, anytime
 • Why swapping foods like rice, pasta, potatoes, or vegetables doesn’t matter nearly as much as you think
 • How to adjust portion sizes to account for calorie differences in protein sources like chicken, steak, and salmon
 • Why fibrous carbs are almost calorically irrelevant—and stressing over them is killing your consistency
 • How to structure your eating around your real life schedule (not someone else’s unrealistic plan)
 • The truth about intermittent fasting, meal timing, and eating frequency
 • And most importantly… how to create a plan you can actually stick to long enough to make it automatic.

Because that’s the goal.

Not perfection.
Consistency.
Once you understand this, you stop starting over.

You stop “falling off.” You stop thinking you need a new diet every 2 weeks.
And you finally build something that lasts.

I’ve done this for over 17 years — not just for myself, but for elite athletes, actors, and everyday people who needed real, sustainable results.

And the ones who succeed?
They’re not the most disciplined.
They’re the ones who stop chasing perfection… and start building consistency.

If you can get through the first 10 days — you’re closer than you think. Most people quit on the 1-yard line. Don’t be one of them. If you’re ready to stop overcomplicating your nutrition and finally take control of it, this video will show you how.

And if you want a system that adapts to YOU — your schedule, your preferences, your goals — I built something called the Flex Factor Meal Plan to do exactly that.

But even if you never use it…
Everything you need to succeed is already in this video.
Watch it. 

Apply it. And give yourself the chance to finally get this right.

If you know someone struggling with their nutrition, share this with them.
Subscribe for more science-based training and nutrition videos that actually work. And for step-by-step programs and tools, head to ATHLEANX.com.
